question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 27.00% on 2026-02-14 for the Metaculus question "Will the United States experience negative GDP growth during Q1, Q2, or Q3 2026?"?
summary: This is a meta-prediction about whether the Metaculus CP for US negative GDP (Q1-Q3 2026) will be above 27% on Feb 14. The CP has been oscillating between 27-28% (rounded) for the past two weeks, with an average of 0.2739 - slightly above the 27% threshold. With 778 forecasters, the CP is very stable. The recent slight upward trend (driven by tariff escalation concerns) and the fact that 92% of recent readings are at or above 27% favor YES. However, the threshold was auto-generated near the CP, so this is structurally close to a coin flip. Strong economic data (Q3 2025 +4.4%, GDPNow Q4 2025 +4.2%) could push the CP down. On balance, the CP is slightly above the threshold and likely to remain there.

factors.description: CP currently at 28% (rounded), average 0.2739 over last week - slightly above the 27% threshold
CP extremely stable with 778 forecasters - 92% of readings at or above 27% in last 2 weeks
Slight upward trend in CP from 25% (mid-Jan) to 28% (early Feb)
Active tariff escalation (Trump threatening Canada, Mexico, China) supports recession fears, maintaining upward pressure
Strong economic fundamentals (Q3 2025 +4.4%, GDPNow Q4 +4.2%, Goldman above-consensus) could push CP down
Meta-question threshold auto-generated near CP - structurally balanced by design, pulls toward 50%
Upcoming economic releases (jobs report ~Feb 6, CPI ~Feb 12) could shift CP in either direction - adds uncertainty

This is a meta-prediction where the CP is sitting right at the 27% threshold. Key considerations:

- **Current position**: CP is at 28% (rounded), with a precise average of ~27.4% over the last week — slightly above the 27.00% threshold
- **Stability**: With 778 forecasters, the CP is very stable. 92% of readings in the last 2 weeks have been at or above 27%
- **Trend**: Slight upward drift from 25% in mid-January to 28% by early February, driven partly by ongoing tariff escalation concerns
- **Counterbalance**: Strong GDP data (Q3 2025 at +4.4%, GDPNow Q4 2025 at +4.2%) and the structurally balanced auto-generated threshold pull toward 50%

The CP is slightly above the threshold and has been stable there for two weeks, giving a modest lean toward YES at 62%.
